zh.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach是William Patric的兒子，於1203年出生在英國英格蘭。他與Beatrix結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1230年享年27歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach是William Patric的儿子，于1203年出生在英国英格兰。他与Beatrix结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1230年享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: Patrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish, Scottish, and English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French, Middle English, and Older Scots personal name Patrick (Old Irish Patraicc), derived from Latin Patricius ‘son of a noble father, member of the patrician class’. This was the name of a Christian saint, a 5th-century Romano-Briton who became the apostle and patron saint of Ireland, and it was largely as a result of his fame that the personal name was so popular from the Middle Ages onward. In Ireland the surname is usually Scottish in origin, from Scottish settlers in Ulster in the 17th century. See also Peden and McPadden , derived from pet forms of Old Irish Patraicc.
Scottish and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Scottish and Irish Gaelic Mac Phádraig ‘son of Patrick’.
English: variant of   Partridge  .

variants.name: Padden
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Páidín ‘son of Páidín’, a pet form of the personal name Pádraig (see Patrick ). Compare McPadden .

English (Devon): habitational name from either Petton in Bampton (Devon), or Paddon in Shebbear (Devon). Petton in Bampton derives from an Old English personal name P(e)atta + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’; Paddon in Shebbear probably has the same etymology, but, due to a lack of early forms, this cannot be certain.

variants.name: McFadden
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phaid(e)in (Scottish) and Mac Pháidín (Irish), patronymics from Gaelic pet forms of the personal name Pádraig, see Patrick .

variants.name: Pate
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Pate, a pet form of Patrick and in Scotland possibly also of Peter . Compare Paton and Peat .
English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English pate ‘head, skull’, perhaps for a man with a bald head. Compare Head .
French (Paté): from Old French paté ‘with paws, pawed’ (from pate ‘paw’), a nickname, applied presumably to a man with large and clumsy hands and feet.

variants.name: Pait
meanings: Scottish:
from the personal name Pait, a short form of Patrick .
nickname for a man with a bald head, from MiddleEnglish pate ‘head, skull’.

variants.name: McFate
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phaid, a patronymicfrom a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name Patrick .

variants.name: McPadden
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brighid, Donal.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Páidín, a patronymic from Páidín, a Gaelic pet form of Patrick . This is the same name as McFadden , without lenition.

variants.name: Patrice
meanings: French: from the personal name Patrice, Latin Patricius (see Patrick ). This surname is also found in the West Indies and Martinique.
Altered form of French   Patry  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Padrick
meanings: Irish: variant of   Patrick  .

variants.name: Peden
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: from a Gaelic pet form of   Patrick  . Compare   Paden   and   Padden  .

variants.name: Paris
meanings: French and English (via Norman French): from the Old French personal name Paris, a variant of Patrice, Patris (from Latin Patricius, from patricius ‘patrician, nobleman’; see Patrick ), which became associated with the name of the Trojan prince Paris in Homer's Iliad (see Parise 1). Compare Parris .
English: habitational name from the city of Paris in France. The placename is taken from the name of a Celtic people, the Parisii, who settled on the banks of the River Seine in pre-Roman times.
Catalan (París): from a shortened form of the personal name Aparici (see Aparicio ).

nameMeanings.name: -Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Castle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Cassell
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name of Norman origin, from Cassel in the Nord department, Flanders, France.
English: sometimes a variant of   Castle  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Caisile, which is probably in origin a variant of Ó Caiside, see Cassidy .

variants.name: Castel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Herve, Jean Michel, Thierry, Yves.  Spanish Jose, Blanco, Carlos, Rafael.
English: variant of   Castle  .
Aragonese, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Castell   3, a cognate of 5 below.

variants.name: Castles
meanings: English: variant of Castle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Armagh and Meath): in Ireland, this may be the English name, but it may also be an Anglicized form of Ó Caisil; see Cashell .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Griffin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a medieval Latinized form, Griffinus, of Griffith .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach was born in 1203, in England, United Kingdom as the son of William Patric. He married Beatrix. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1230, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach est né en 1203, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William Patric. Il a épousé Beatrix. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1230, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach wurde 1203 in England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William Patric geboren. Er hat Beatrix geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1230 im Alter von 27 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach è nato il nel 1203 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di William Patric. Lui sposato Beatrix. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1230, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.Shocklache, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelachは1203年にイギリスイギリスでWilliam Patricの息子として生まれた。Beatrixと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1230年に27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach(은)는 1203년 영국 잉글랜드에서 William Patric의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Beatrix(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1230년 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach nasceu em 1203, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de William Patric. Ele casou-se com Beatrix. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1230, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach родился в 1203 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали William Patric. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrix. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1230 г. в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Griffin Patrick -Lord of Castle Shokelach nació en 1203, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de William Patric. Se casó con Beatrix. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1230, a la edad de 27 años.
